title: Eriosema vanderystii
taxonomy: Eriosema vanderystii is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Eriosema, which is a group of legumes commonly found in tropical Africa. The classification of Eriosema vanderystii is further divided into the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Phaseoleae. Within the tribe Phaseoleae, Eriosema vanderystii is part of the subtribe Phaseolinae. This classification helps to identify the plant's relationships and characteristics within the pea family.
uses: Eriosema vanderystii is reportedly used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves are used to treat fever and rheumatism. The plant is also used to treat stomach problems and as an antiseptic. In some cultures, it is used to treat snake bites. Additionally, it is said to have antibacterial properties.

intro: Eriosema vanderystii, also known as the African ironplant, is a flowering plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae). The African ironplant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Eriosema vanderystii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eriosema vanderystii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or syrup, and is also used in the form of a tincture or ointment to treat various ailments.
